turns.thought: [OBSERVE] The swarm is stalled with no improvement for 200 iterations. Diversity is essentially zero (normalized diversity 7.44e-18, swarm radius 1.72e-15). Velocity means and std are near zero, all particles have zero velocity (velocity_zero_ratio=1.0). Fitness values are all identical to within 2.8e-14 (gbest=103.307, mean=103.307, worst=103.307). Boundary hit ratio is 0, so particles are not at edges. The swarm has collapsed into a single point configuration, a classical premature convergence scenario. | [DIAGNOSE] The root cause is a total loss of exploration capacity: the swarm has converged to a single local optimum (fitness ~103) far from the global minimum (f=0). All particles share the same position and zero velocity. The local basin appears to be a deceptive or flat region where the PSO's exploitation mechanism was unable to descend further and diversity vanished. This is not just slow progress—it is a complete stagnation that will never improve without outside intervention. | [STRATEGIZE] The immediate goal is to restore swarm diversity while preserving the best-known position. I will reset a large fraction of the worst particles to random positions, reset their velocities, and increase inertia weight to promote exploration. This is better than local mutation methods (Gaussian or Lévy) because the swarm is at a single point; local perturbations around that point would likely fall back into the same basin given the absence of gradient information. A reset can inject particles into entirely new regions, potentially discovering better basins, while retaining the current gbest as a memory of the best solution so far.

The diagnosis of premature convergence was correct. The immediate task is to let the swarm explore and exploit the new diversity.
